- Bachelor's degree or equivalent in business, management, real estate, or another related field
- Experience in large complex deal negotiations with a successful track record
- Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Do you have the business savvy and the technical background necessary to help establish Amazon as a key technology platform provider?

In this role you will be part of a team that drives AWS's data center contract negotiations, with a focus on colocation projects. You will play a crucial part in expanding our data center footprint while optimizing costs, keeping apace with evolving data center design, and ensuring operational excellence. The position encompasses comprehensive ownership of both new and existing colocation agreements, including pricing structures, terms, and service level agreements, while developing and implementing market expansion strategies across existing and new markets.

The role focuses on structuring deals and serves as the primary contact with colocation providers. The position requires strong market analysis capabilities to evaluate conditions, pricing trends, and competitive landscapes, along with the ability to create and present compelling business cases to senior leadership. Success demands excellence in relationship building, stakeholder management, and driving complex multi-million dollar negotiations while maintaining AWS's competitive advantage in the global cloud infrastructure market. You will work closely with Legal, Finance, Engineering, Operations, and Project Management teams. The role requires a self-starter who is a team player and is not afraid to keep learning.



Key job responsibilities
• Serve as a key member of the team in driving data center deals and overall infrastructure strategy for target regions
• Understand the supplier base (both Colocation providers and Real Estate developers), develop a strong understanding of market dynamics and activity, and develop long-term strategic partnerships in support of our key markets
• Set business development plans in-line with the larger organization strategic direction
• Negotiate and close multi-million dollar contracts to support capacity expansion
• Understand the technical requirements of our engineering teams and negotiate solutions that meet technical requirements as well as the business objectives
• Ownership of business case creation and presentation to leadership (including commercial, technical and risk profile)
• Prepare and give project reviews to the senior management team regarding progress and deal execution roadblocks
• Ability to work in a high-pressure environment and pivot on the fly to changing requirements to manage competing priorities simultaneously
• Continue to build a culture of speed, flexibility and frugality with all business partners
• Develop and implement new mechanisms to improve our customer experience
• Business Travel: 10-20%

About the team
About AWS
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
INSLP (Security & Loss Prevention) is hiring an SLP Specialist to support growth of the “SLP MDR Concessions Abuse Program” and help preventing losses via identification of abusive entity. The program is aimed at identifying and eliminating Concessions Abuse while preserving customer trust.
The primary objective of the SLP Specialist is to provide security services, investigation and asset protection in designated area of responsibility, assist in mitigating product loss, and maintain customer satisfaction in accordance with INSLP guidelines and policies. The role is cross-functional and requires deep collaboration and influencing ability with stakeholders from business and corporate functions. SLP Specialist's day-to-day activities involve planning, organizing, coordinating, implementing and executing process and procedures laid down by the organization and the SLP function.
As a part of the SLP MDR Concessions Abuse program, the SLP Specialist is required to deep dive into the Concessions request raised by customer, and conduct end-to-end investigation across customer, DA/network and seller to identify the abusive entity. SLP has outlined frameworks for investigation, and the SLP Specialist is bound to adhere to the investigation guidelines. Along with primarily supporting on Concessions investigations, the SLP Specialist will be required to provide on-ground intel useful in uncovering abuse MOs. In order to ensure network integrity, the SLP Specialist will be required to conduct risk assessment and process audit for the respective sites and work in accordance with the security policies and guidelines.

Key job responsibilities
Security Operation
• Implementation and follow-up of Loss Prevention Plan in MDR Concessions

Audits and Compliance
• Conducting SLP audits and implementing SOPs
• Surprise check and reports at LM (Last Mile) stations
• Keeping the plans and SOPs updated
• Periodical check of documentation
• Adhering to SLAs defined for the SLP MDR Concessions Abuse Program

Process Improvement and Loss Prevention
• Process review, gap analysis and implementation of necessary improvements
• Weekly, monthly and quarterly assessments
• Loss prevention reporting
• Working on feedbacks received from internal and external stakeholders
• Follow up and completion of CAPA
• Recommending loss prevention initiatives based on on-ground intel

Loss Prevention Analysis and Investigation
• Work closely with the SLP and CS teams to analyze the loss trends in MDR Concessions
• Weekly loss trend analysis, identifying defects / abusive entities and working towards loss reduction
• Conduct investigations by working closely with CS and support functions in SLP and Ops
• Meeting the weekly investigation targets and submitting investigation reports
• Sharing RCAs and PTG initiatives on shrink and unmet goals

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.


Key job responsibilities
The responsibilities of the ACx Commissioning Engineer (CxE) include, but are not limited to, oversight and coordination of multi-discipline division of work related to pre-commissioning and commissioning of Amazon critical infrastructure construction and special projects.
• The CxE is responsible for supporting and supervising vendors, contractors and other teams involved in the construction and commissioning of various systems including medium and low voltage power systems, Generators, UPS, HVAC, Chilled Water Systems, Fuel Transfer Systems, BMS and EPMS systems.
• They provides multi-discipline input into the scheduling, designing and planning of projects. In addition, the CxE will be required to review all multi-discipline integrated systems testing and commissioning documents to ensure they are compliant with the basis of design, specifications and standards of the project.
• It is the CxE's responsibility to ensure that all commissioning procedures follow the appropriate safety practices, policies and procedures. He or she enthusiastically participates in all project and company safety programs.
• The CxE must also audit commissioning activities continuously to ensure compliance with environmental, quality, and safety requirements.
• The CxE proactively informs the ACx Regional Manager and along with the ACx team to ensure that the applicable test documents are created in accordance with all commissioning requirements so that this is aligned with the global standards and practices.
• Following the AWS project reporting format, the CxE is responsible for weekly reporting of commissioning progress and metrics for assigned project.
• You will work with deadlines across multiple projects simultaneously, while maintaining project budgets according to financial metrics
• Willingness to travel domestic and internationally for up to 50% of the time

- Experience exceeding sales targets using a consultative, solutions-focused approach or equivalent

jobs.description:
Amazon Ads' Large Customer Sales (LCS) organization is seeking a Senior Account Executive (L6) to own and grow a portfolio of large, complex advertising clients and prospects. In this role, you will serve as the primary relationship owner and strategic lead for a set of upper-tier advertisers and their agency partners, driving sustained media investment and delivering measurable business outcomes for clients and Amazon Ads.

LCS Senior Account Executives are objective-based sellers (OBS) who cultivate a deep understanding of customers' needs and aspirations, then create measurable, tailored multi-product Amazon Ads solutions — spanning display, online video, Streaming TV (STV), Audio, Sponsored Ads, AdTech, and Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC).

Key job responsibilities
Own and lead the overall advertiser relationship and strategy, including account planning and direct advertiser agreements, for a set of complex, upper-tier accounts
Consistently meet and exceed revenue targets by securing sustained media investment partnerships and making responsive recommendations that expand the breadth and depth of Amazon Ads products adopted across client objectives and divisions
Deliver structured, objective-based long-term plans that drive growth and improve the advertiser experience (AX)
Build and maintain trusted relationships with senior client and agency stakeholders (VP/Director level and above) who influence or control decision-making within the advertiser and/or agency team
Set customer objective-based priorities for the cross-functional account team, orchestrating internal partner teams (Account Managers, Campaign Creative Managers, Programmatic Solutions Consultants, Analytics & Insights, AdTech, Product Sales Specialists, etc.)
Develop data-driven insights, advertiser/agency education, and an industry perspective to build effective client relationships
Communicate clearly and persuasively with client and agency senior leadership; contribute to and potentially own cadenced reporting and narratives (WBR, MBR, QBR, 2-pager, 6-pager, PR/FAQ)
Improve, streamline, and eliminate excess processes; drive efficiencies and develop best practices, approaches, and playbooks across your vertical
Influence the sales organization, partner teams, and senior leaders; advocate on behalf of the customer within Amazon
Participate in and/or lead established and emerging strategic initiatives; begin to influence partner team roadmaps

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Knowledge of mechanical production processes (e.g., milling, turning, welding…)
- Knowledge of ERP systems, SAP is a plus
- Experience in Microsoft Office
- Communication skills in English, both verbal and written
jobs.description:
At Amazon, we strive to be the most customer-centric company on earth. Achieving that goal requires exceptionally talented, curious, and driven people. Every day, millions of customers rely on Amazon to deliver products quickly, safely, and efficiently.

Behind this experience is cutting-edge automation.

Our Mechatronics team develops the next generation of automation technologies that power Amazon’s global fulfillment and delivery network. From packaging and labeling systems to advanced sortation and conveyance solutions, we design and build the systems that enable speed, safety, and sustainability at scale.

We are a global innovation organization, with R&D labs in Hamme (Belgium), Boston (USA), and Milan (Italy). At our Hamme lab, ideas quickly move from concept to prototype to production.

To support this innovation, we are looking for a Work Preparator (Werkvoorbereider / Production Planner / Manufacturing Planner / Production Coordinator) to join our production team in Hamme.

The Role

As a Work Preparator, you are the key link between engineering, production, and external suppliers. You ensure that innovative designs from our engineering teams are translated into efficient, manufacturable production processes.

You prepare, plan, and coordinate the production of mechanical components within a machining environment that includes turning, milling, welding, and sheet metal fabrication.

Your work directly enables our teams to transform new automation concepts into real systems used across Amazon’s global operations.

Key job responsibilities
Production Planning & Coordination

Plan and monitor internal production activities across machining processes (CNC turning, milling, welding, sheet metal).

Define detailed production routings and workflows to guide the shop floor.

Coordinate with external suppliers to ensure timely deliveries aligned with project milestones.

Manage raw material orders and monitor stock levels for internal manufacturing.

Engineering Collaboration

Partner closely with engineers early in the design phase to assess manufacturability of components.

Identify potential production challenges and define practical solutions.

Translate technical drawings into optimized machining and fabrication processes.

Project Coordination

Represent the production department within multiple cross-functional development projects.

Contribute production expertise to project planning and scheduling.

Ensure new projects are fully prepared before entering production.

Proactively identify and escalate potential bottlenecks or delivery risks.

Production Analysis & Continuous Improvement

Perform pre-calculations of production times to support accurate planning.

Conduct post-calculations to compare planned versus actual performance.

Analyze production data and identify opportunities for efficiency improvements.

Basic Qualifications

Strong knowledge of machining and fabrication processes:

CNC Turning

CNC Milling

Welding (multiple techniques)

Sheet metal fabrication

Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and manufacturing documentation

Experience working with ERP systems (SAP experience is a strong advantage)

Understanding of production planning, capacity management, and scheduling

Strong organizational and communication skills

Ability to work across engineering, production, and supplier teams

About the team
At Amazon Mechatronics in Hamme, you will:

Work on cutting-edge automation technologies used across Amazon’s global network

Collaborate with international engineering teams and industry experts

See your work move rapidly from concept to real machines

Be part of a hands-on innovation environment where engineering and production meet

Contribute to building the future of warehouse automation

jobs.description:
Do you have a passion for Startups? Do you have a passion for helping early startup companies grow and thrive? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is looking for an experienced and motivated technical manager to coach, grow, and partner with skilled Startups Solutions Architects focusing on a wide range of customers. Working in collaboration with our sales teams, you will drive growth across a broad set of customers in this segment. If you think you have what it takes to lead the best in the industry, AWS is hiring Managers for our Solutions Architects!

As a Sr. Solutions Architecture Manager for India Startups, you will lead a team of skilled Solutions Architects who partner with early-stage to growth-stage startups across India. You will be hands-on, working directly with customers on significantly complex technical challenges while coaching your team to elevate their impact. You should bring proven strategic thinking, deep technical expertise, and demonstrated ability to influence across organizations.

You will need to be technically capable and credible in your own right—able to architect solutions to significantly complex problems, decompose technical complexity into straightforward solutions, and serve as a trusted advisor to startup founders and CTOs. Simultaneously, you must be effective in developing the customer-facing and technical skills of your experienced team members.

In addition to technical depth, you should have a solid understanding of the startup journey—the unique challenges startups face and how technology can empower their businesses. You will work directly with a variety of startups, helping them make the best technical decisions as they scale, while building your team's capability to do the same.

Key job responsibilities
Customer Engagement & Technical Leadership
- Lead critical customer engagements including cloud adoption and migration programs, and complex technical challenges with high-stakes outcomes
- Serve as a trusted technical advisor to startup founders and senior business and technical leaders, operating at a strategic level and creating opportunities for your team to act as trusted advisors
- Apply deep technical understanding across multiple domains to invent, evolve, and simplify solutions that drive customer success

Team Leadership & Development
- Coach and develop experienced Solutions Architects to elevate their technical capabilities and strategic advisory abilities
- Build a high-performing team through effective hiring, onboarding, and continuous skill development
- Foster a culture of technical excellence and customer obsession
- Maintain a high bar for operational excellence; define and maintain appropriate cadences for inspecting the business in your area

Strategic Impact & Collaboration
- Champion your area’s customer needs with senior leaders within AWS
- Bridge customer needs with AWS product teams, ensuring customer feedback drives product improvements
- Partner with senior leaders within AWS sales, investor management, specialist, and engineering teams to deliver comprehensive solutions and clear obstacles to customer success

About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
Amazon Hub (formerly I Have Space program) is seeking an experienced Program Manager to drive distribution expansion through strategic lead generation and store onboarding initiatives. This role will be instrumental in expanding our last-mile delivery network by identifying, engaging, and onboarding retail partners who serve as delivery points for Amazon customers.

Key job responsibilities
### Lead Generation & Distribution Expansion
- Develop and execute comprehensive lead generation strategies to identify potential Amazon Hub partner locations
- Build and maintain a robust pipeline of prospective retail partners across assigned territories
- Analyze market data and demographics to prioritize high-value expansion opportunities
- Set and achieve quarterly and annual store onboarding targets
### Go-to-Market Strategy & Execution
- Design and implement go-to-market approaches tailored to different retail segments and geographic markets
- Create compelling value propositions that resonate with potential partner stores
- Develop sales collateral, pitch decks, and training materials for field teams
- Test and iterate on outreach strategies to optimize conversion rates
### Stakeholder Engagement & Field Operations
- Partner closely with last-mile station teams including Cluster Managers, Site Leads, team leads, and ADEs (Account development executives)
- Enable and empower field teams to conduct effective store visits and partner conversations
- Conduct joint field visits to model best practices and provide hands-on coaching
- Build strong cross-functional relationships to ensure seamless program execution
### Program Management & Operations
- Own end-to-end onboarding process from initial contact through store activation
- Track and report on key performance metrics including pipeline health, conversion rates, and time-to-activation
- Identify and remove blockers that impede store onboarding progress
- Implement process improvements to scale operations efficiently
### Data Analysis & Reporting
- Leverage data analytics to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement
- Create executive-level dashboards and reports on program performance
- Conduct post-mortems on successful and unsuccessful onboarding attempts
- Use insights to continuously refine lead generation and conversion strategies

jobs.description:
We are looking for a dynamic, organized and self-starting Planning and Ops manager who can drive innovation, set up the processes and execute in the space of operational and supply chain excellence in Amazon India business. This becomes all the more challenging in India where the mode of business is marketplace.
You will own operational performance of sellers / vendors on marketplace, influence FC Ops processes and work with various stakeholders to protect vendor & customer experience. You will also be in-charge of driving system/process improvements working with Seller ISMs, SC Ops teams, vendor operations, and delivering on key operation programs which makes the Amazon India business sustainable and profitable in the long term. You will create, write and review business cases, perform supporting research and analysis, and develop solutions from conceptualization to execution and monitoring.
This position requires strong self-motivation, self-starting and project management skills. The ideal candidate will have the ability to work closely with all the category leaders, planning and forecasting managers, operations team, and technology teams to develop strategy, dashboards, and modes of execution to solve complex inbound operation problems. The candidate will be working with cross-functional teams to deliver on seller / vendor operational performance along with key operations improvement programs.



Key job responsibilities
 Ensure seller / vendor operational performance delivery inline with targets
 Identify and articulate strategic importance of metrics as a basis for managing tradeoffs, improving the customer experience and making decisions with internal and external stakeholders
 Work closely with vendor operations teams on strategies to reduce cost, lead time, and waste across the end-to-end supply chain
 Define, prioritize, and monitor to execution the programs/projects to achieve targets be delivered on time
 Create, write and review business cases, perform supporting research and analysis, write specifications, and drive the product development schedule from strategy to execution.
 Retrieve and analyze data using Excel, Access, SQL or other data management systems
 Monitor project execution and ensure that the project delivery is to the appropriate levels of quality and in line with its target date, ensuring the overall integrity within the program
 Work with Cross-functional teams to deliver operational performance / projects
Successful candidates will be high analytical and having strong deep dive bias, excellent communicators, strong influencers, curious, highly motivated and effective within distributed teams.
The position is located in Bangalore, India.


A day in the life
Driving Supply Planning with Sellers to ensure right balance of inbounds are done so that availability isn't impacted and inbound labour is optimized.

- 1+ years of data analytics or automation experience
- 1+ years of capacity planning, operations planning, business analysis or similar experience

jobs.description:
Are you customer obsessed, flexible, smart and analytical, strategic yet execution focused, hungry and passionate about e-commerce, experienced, and entrepreneurial leader with a strong work ethic? If yes, this opportunity will appeal to you.

The ideal candidate will be enthusiastic about managing challenging projects across multiple teams and locations. We are looking for a Business Analyst who shares Amazon's passion for the customer—someone who understands the Engineering and Business both. This role requires an individual with excellent understanding of SQL and query development, good business acumen and the ability to work with business, program and product teams. The successful candidate will be a self-starter comfortable with ambiguity, with strong attention to detail, an ability to work in a fast-paced and ever-changing environment, and driven by a desire to innovate in this space.

To be successful in this role you should have superior communication, presentation and organizational skills. Operating in a fast-moving and sometimes ambiguous environment you will work autonomously taking control and responsibility for achieving the objectives of the role. This role provides opportunities to develop original ideas, approaches, and solutions in a competitive and ever changing business climate. You will also partner with product management teams as well to identify long term features and programs to improve the seller and customer experience on the Amazon platform.

Key job responsibilities
1. Strong SQL & data analysis skills (should be very comfortable writing ETL queries in Datanet/Cradle)
2. Highly proficient with MS Excel
3. Enabling effective decision making by retrieving and aggregating data from multiple sources and compiling it into a digestible and actionable format
4. Analyzing and solving business problems with focus on understanding root causes and driving forward-looking opportunities
5. Designing new metrics and enhance existing metrics to support the future state of business processes and ensure sustainability
6. Understanding tools and processes and driving seller adoption
7. Communicating complex analysis and insights to stakeholders and business leaders, both verbally and in writing.
